audit_status.source_base: Greek Revelation text tradition with Exodus/Joel/abyss imagery; draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for fifth and sixth trumpets, abyss imagery, locust army symbols, unrepentance.

verses.restored: The fifth messenger trumpeted, and I saw a star fallen from heaven to earth; to him was given the key of the pit of the abyss.
He opened the pit of the abyss, and smoke went up like smoke of great furnace, and sun and air were darkened from the smoke.
From the smoke locusts came out onto earth, and authority was given them like authority of scorpions of earth.
They were told not to harm grass, green thing, or tree, but only humans without seal of Divine on their foreheads.
It was given them not to kill but to torment five months; their torment like torment of scorpion when it strikes a human.
In those days humans will seek death and not find it; they will desire to die, and death flees from them.
The likenesses of locusts were like horses prepared for war; on heads like crowns of gold, faces like human faces.
They had hair like women's hair, and teeth like lions' teeth.
They had breastplates like iron, and sound of wings like sound of many chariots with horses running into battle.
They have tails like scorpions and stings; in their tails is authority to harm humans five months.
They have over them king, messenger of abyss; his name in Hebrew is Abaddon, and in Greek he has name Apollyon.
The first woe went away; look, two woes still come after these.
The sixth messenger trumpeted, and I heard one voice from four horns of golden altar before the Divine,
saying to the sixth messenger with trumpet: Release the four messengers bound at the great river Euphrates.
And the four messengers were released, prepared for hour, day, month, and year, so that a third of humans might be killed.
Number of armies of horsemen was two myriads of myriads; I heard their number.

And thus I saw horses in the vision and those sitting on them, having breastplates fire-colored, hyacinth, and sulfur-like; heads of horses like heads of lions, and from their mouths come fire, smoke, and sulfur.
By these three plagues a third of humans were killed: from fire, smoke, and sulfur coming from their mouths.
For authority of horses is in their mouths and tails; their tails are like serpents, having heads, and with them they harm.

The rest of humans, who were not killed by these plagues, did not turn from works of their hands, so as not to worship daimons and idols of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood, which can neither see nor hear nor walk.
And they did not turn from murders, sorceries, sexual misuses, nor thefts.

translation_notes: Abyss/locust imagery is symbolic plague/anti-creation imagery; avoid literal monster speculation.
The chapter's moral endpoint is unrepentant idolatry and works of hands.
safety_note: Do not use Revelation 9 to incite fear, self-harm, or target people. Treat the images as symbolic exposure of destructive powers and idolatrous systems.
